brands.name: Valvoline Instant Oil Change
brands.notes: Quick-lube automotive service brand operating 2,000+ North American locations; franchise development site is viocfranchise.com. System is roughly half company-owned, so new franchise opportunities are a smaller subset of total units.

riskFlags.title: Large company-owned share limits franchise availability
riskFlags.detail: Historically a substantial portion of VIOC service centers are company-owned (one cited snapshot: 636 franchised vs 264 company-owned of 900 U.S. centers; more recent reporting puts nearly half of stores company-owned), so available franchise markets are constrained.

riskFlags.title: Very wide total-investment range and high capital ceiling
riskFlags.detail: FDD-cited total investment spans from roughly $192K up to as high as $3.48M depending on real estate/build scenario; ground-up centers commonly run $821K-$2.1M, a high capital requirement for the category.

nationwide.source: Valvoline Instant Oil Change official franchise site (viocfranchise.com)
nationwide.note: Site states '2,000+ Stores Nationwide' and that the brand is 'nationwide, but valuable markets are still on the table'; notes 15 states regulate franchise sales and require pre-sale registration.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Low end of full FDD range; conversion of existing facility. Ground-up centers commonly start ~$821K.

totalInvestmentMax.note: High end of full FDD range including real estate; typical ground-up ceiling cited ~$2.1M.

royaltyPercent.note: Sources conflict: 6% royalty most commonly cited for the 2024 FDD; some older summaries report 4%. Used most recent/common figure.

adFundPercent.note: Advertising/marketing contribution; one source cites 6%. Conflict noted.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Approximate average unit volume from public/aggregator sources; not confirmed as a direct FDD Item 19 disclosure. Treat as low confidence.

yearFounded.note: VIOC concept created 1986 via purchase of Rapid Oil Change; parent Valvoline brand dates to 1866.

totalUnits.note: '2,000+ stores nationwide' including company-owned and franchised; franchised-only count is a smaller subset.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Development agreements require a minimum of at least three service centers.

brands.name: Christian Brothers Automotive
brands.notes: Faith-based full-service auto repair franchise with one of the highest average unit volumes in the category (~$2.8M AUV) and a profit-split royalty model. Franchise development site is christianbrothersfranchise.com.

riskFlags.title: Unusual 50% profit-split royalty structure
riskFlags.detail: Rather than a flat percent-of-sales royalty, CBA takes 50% of monthly 'Split Profits' for the full term and all renewals; prospective franchisees must model this carefully as it differs sharply from standard category royalties.

riskFlags.title: Wide spread in franchisee owner benefit
riskFlags.detail: Item 19 shows top-20% franchisees averaging ~$609,691 total owner benefit vs bottom-20% averaging ~$132,289, indicating substantial performance variability despite a high system-wide AUV.

riskFlags.title: FDD discloses litigation/bankruptcy history
riskFlags.detail: Third-party due-diligence sources note CBA discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy items in its FDD that prospects should review; no major systemic franchisee-vs-franchisor litigation pattern surfaced and the brand states it has never closed a location.

nationwide.source: Christian Brothers Automotive official franchise site
nationwide.note: Site cites 335+ locations 'across the nation' and an 'Available Markets'/'Find Available Territories' section for nationwide growth markets.

adFundPercent.note: National marketing program capped at up to 5% of prior-year average annual revenue.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19 average unit volume across 265 franchisee-owned locations open the full year 2023; median total owner benefit $302,298.

totalUnits.note: Franchised locations at end of 2023; zero company-owned. Site cites 335+ by 2024-2025.

veteranDiscount.note: IFA VetFran discount reduces franchise fee to $121,500.

isGrowing.note: Unit count grew from ~280 (end 2023) toward 335+ per franchise site.

brands.name: Sylvan Learning
brands.notes: Supplemental K-12 tutoring franchise (now part of Unleashed Brands after a 2024 acquisition) with ~475+ franchised territories. Carries a notably high 16% royalty plus 8% advertising minimum.

riskFlags.title: High combined royalty + ad burden
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is 16% of monthly gross revenues (minimum $32,000/yr) plus an 8% advertising/local minimum (capped $40,000/territory/yr) - among the highest fee loads in the education category.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee bankruptcy and consumer-refund complaints
riskFlags.detail: A Sylvan franchise owner filed bankruptcy owing $600K+; parents reported canceled sessions, inability to book tutors, and refused refunds on prepaid/financed packages, illustrating unit-level execution and consumer-protection risk.

riskFlags.title: Parent company (Unleashed Brands) franchisee litigation
riskFlags.detail: Unleashed Brands, which acquired Sylvan in 2024, has faced franchisee lawsuits alleging it downplayed operational demands and pushed owners into multiple concepts; an arbitrator ruled in Unleashed's favor in one case, but disputes across its brands are ongoing.

statesOpen.note: Sylvan franchise 'Available Markets' page lists most U.S. states open for development; representative state.
Listed among open states on Sylvan franchise available-territories page.
Listed among open states on Sylvan franchise available-territories page.
Listed among open states on Sylvan franchise available-territories page.
Sylvan franchise available-territories page lists nearly all U.S. states (AL-WY) as open; OH representative.

royaltyPercent.note: 2025 FDD royalty 11-12% plus separate 5% National Advertising Fund. Prior batch value 16 superseded by verification audit 2026-06-11.

adFundPercent.note: Advertising minimum 8% of monthly gross revenues, capped $40,000/territory/year.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19 average gross revenues across territories operating continuously 2022-2023; median lower (quartiles ranged ~$179K bottom to ~$689K top).

totalUnits.note: 474 franchised + 5 company-owned in 2024; modest net growth (+6 franchised).

brands.name: Huntington Learning Center
brands.notes: Tutoring and test-prep franchise founded 1977. Item 19 average gross revenue ~$522K, but the system has contracted in recent years (franchised units declined 285 to 276, 2020-2022) and has a history of franchisee litigation.

riskFlags.title: Unit contraction
riskFlags.detail: Franchised outlets declined from 285 (2020) to 276 (2022) and company-owned from 16 to 10; longer-term contraction is steeper (342 U.S. franchises in 2008 to ~250 by 2011), signaling a shrinking system.
riskFlags.source: FranchiseChatter FDD Talk (2024 review) / Unhappy Franchisee

riskFlags.title: Franchisee lawsuits alleging interference and misleading earnings
riskFlags.detail: Franchisee Joe Lieberman sued claiming Huntington's interference forced his center to close in 2018; a separate Arizona franchisee (Tozzo) alleged a brochure deceptively implied significant returns while 'nearly all' Arizona franchisees were in financial extremis.

riskFlags.title: Wide revenue dispersion
riskFlags.detail: Item 19 gross revenue ranged from $59,244 (lowest) to $2,692,251 (highest) with median ($449,598) well below average ($522,068), indicating many centers underperform the headline average.

totalInvestmentMax.note: 2025 FDD Item 7 maximum $302,000. Prior batch value 264028 superseded by verification audit 2026-06-11.

adFundPercent.note: 2% of Huntington services/eTutoring revenue ($500/mo min) plus a $57,000/yr minimum advertising requirement.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19 average gross revenue across 257 franchised centers open during 2021 or before; median $449,598.

totalUnits.note: 276 franchised + 10 company-owned (2022 FDD data); declining trend.

brands.name: Primrose Schools
brands.notes: Premium early-education/childcare franchise with very high AUV (~$2.6M) and large capital requirements (build-to-suit). 100% franchised, growing system, but childcare category carries inherent child-safety liability exposure.

riskFlags.title: Child-safety incidents and franchise terminations
riskFlags.detail: Multiple locations lost state licenses and had franchise agreements terminated over child-abuse/neglect allegations (e.g., Ballantyne NC, Briargate CO); a Jacksonville FL family won an $8M verdict over a child choking incident, highlighting category liability exposure.
riskFlags.source: Queen City News / Yahoo News / KRDO

riskFlags.title: Very high capital requirement and investment ceiling
riskFlags.detail: Total investment ranges roughly $743K to $1.5M for build-to-suit (third-party real-estate scenarios cited up to ~$8.6M), among the highest in the education/childcare category and a barrier for many franchisees.

nationwide.note: Franchising page promotes nationwide development with 'Immediate Real Estate Sites' and recent openings across multiple states; 558 franchised schools open as of end of 2025. No state-level restriction listed.

franchiseFee.note: Franchise fee ranges $50,000-$80,000; lower bound used. Separate $35,000 initial training fee.

totalInvestmentMax.note: 2025 FDD max $8,595,000 across all development programs; build-to-suit-only max is $1,525,000. Prior batch value 1525000 superseded by verification audit 2026-06-11.

adFundPercent.note: 2% brand fund plus 1% local advertising and 1% cooperative contributions.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19 average gross revenue across 477 active facilities (2023 data); median $2,563,585; average EBITDA $445,780 (16%) across 408 reporting facilities.

totalUnits.note: Franchised locations end of 2023 (100% franchised); +22 net in 2023. Official site cites 558 by end of 2025.

isGrowing.note: Net +22 franchised units in 2023; growth to 558 by end of 2025.
